title: Data analytics path handout (modules 7–9)
subtitle: Pipeline, MASTER prompt and the 8-step project – print it or keep it nearby while you work.
pipelineSteps: Data collection – name the sources, topic and context.
Data preparation – clean formats, missing values and duplicates.
Exploratory analysis (EDA) – check patterns, outliers and questions worth testing.
Models and methods – choose the analysis method based on the question.
Visualisation – choose charts and metrics a manager can understand.
Publishing – turn insights into a clear report or decision.
masterPromptSteps: Data sources – state where the information comes from.
Data structure – describe tables, fields or documents.
Cleaning – remove duplicates, align formats and mark gaps.
Exploratory analysis (EDA) – ask for patterns, anomalies and hypotheses.
Visualisations – ask for chart ideas and what each one should show.
Insights – explain what the data means for the business or team.
Forecasts – name possible scenarios and risks.
workflowSteps: Collect data sources about the company, sector or question.
Prepare the methodology prompt: what to analyse and which metrics to use.
Prepare a deep research prompt that works across several AI tools.
Run the same research in 2–4 AI tools and save the outputs.
Clean the outputs: remove duplicates and align dates and numbers.
Merge the outputs into one structured document.
Ask for a visualisation specification: charts, colours and metrics.
Generate a simple dashboard or a clear executive summary.
reflectionSummary: Repeat the 8 steps with another topic. Ask yourself: what did I create, what does it mean for my work and what is the next single action?
nextPageIntro: One workflow cycle = one finished project. Next, deepen the template or choose the next step in the Prompt Anatomy ecosystem.
footerText: Prompt Anatomy – prompt structure training. © Course material.
